title: Activate a new device
content:
## How to activate your new T-Mobile device

Welcome to T-Mobile! Here's how to get your new device up and running.

### If you have a SIM card

1. Power off your new device
2. Insert the T-Mobile SIM card into the SIM tray
3. Power on the device
4. Follow the on-screen setup prompts
5. Your device should automatically connect to the T-Mobile network

### If you have an eSIM

1. Power on your new device and connect to Wi-Fi
2. Open the T-Mobile app or visit t-mobile.com
3. Sign in with your T-Mobile ID
4. Follow the eSIM activation prompts
5. Restart your device when prompted

### Troubleshooting activation

If your device doesn't activate automatically, try these steps:

- Restart your device
- Make sure you're in an area with T-Mobile coverage
- Check that your SIM card is properly inserted
- Call 611 from another phone or visit a T-Mobile store for assistance

Activation typically completes within a few minutes, but in some cases may take up to 24 hours.

title: Switch to T-Mobile from another carrier
content:
## Switching to T-Mobile

Switching to T-Mobile is easy, and we'll help you every step of the way.

### Before you switch

- **Don't cancel** your current service until the switch is complete
- Make sure your current device is paid off or unlocked
- Have your current account number and PIN ready
- Check device compatibility at t-mobile.com/resources/bring-your-own-phone

### How to switch

1. **Choose a plan** at t-mobile.com or visit a T-Mobile store
2. **Port your number** — we'll transfer your existing phone number from your old carrier
3. **Activate your device** — insert your new SIM card or set up eSIM
4. **Return or trade in** your old device if applicable

### Keep your phone number

When you port your number to T-Mobile, your old carrier will automatically cancel your service on that line. This usually happens within a few hours.

### Switching deals

T-Mobile frequently offers switching incentives including:
- Device credits when you trade in your old phone
- Bill credits to cover early termination fees from your old carrier
- Free additional lines for a limited time

Visit t-mobile.com/offers for current switching deals.

title: Port your phone number to T-Mobile
content:
## Porting your number to T-Mobile

Keep your existing phone number when you switch to T-Mobile.

### What you'll need

- Your current carrier's **account number**
- Your current carrier's **account PIN or password**
- The **billing address** on your current account
- Your **phone number** to transfer

### How to port your number

1. Sign in to your T-Mobile account or the T-Mobile app
2. Go to **Account > Line Settings > Transfer number**
3. Enter your current carrier information
4. Confirm the transfer request
5. Keep your old phone active until the port completes

### Important tips

- **Do not cancel** your old service before the port is complete — this can cause you to lose your number
- Porting typically takes **2-4 hours** but can take up to **24 hours**
- You may lose service briefly during the transfer
- Once the port is complete, your old carrier service will automatically cancel

### Port status

Check the status of your port in the T-Mobile app under **Account > Line Settings**, or call T-Mobile customer care at 1-800-937-8997.

title: Set up the T-Mobile app
content:
## Getting started with the T-Mobile app

The T-Mobile app is the easiest way to manage your account, pay your bill, and get support.

### Download the app

- **iPhone**: Download from the App Store
- **Android**: Download from Google Play Store

### Create your T-Mobile ID

1. Open the T-Mobile app
2. Tap **Sign Up**
3. Enter your T-Mobile phone number
4. Create a password
5. Verify your identity via text message

### What you can do in the app

- **Pay your bill** and set up AutoPay
- **Check data usage** for each line on your account
- **Change your plan** or add features
- **Contact support** via chat, call, or messaging
- **View and manage** your device and account details
- **Shop for deals** on devices and accessories
- **Access T-Mobile Tuesdays** rewards

### Troubleshooting

If you're having trouble with the app:
- Make sure you have the latest version installed
- Try clearing the app cache
- Uninstall and reinstall the app
- Verify your T-Mobile ID credentials at my.t-mobile.com

title: Create a T-Mobile ID
content:
## Creating your T-Mobile ID

Your T-Mobile ID is your login for managing your account online and in the T-Mobile app.

### How to create a T-Mobile ID

1. Go to **my.t-mobile.com** or open the T-Mobile app
2. Click **Sign Up** or **Register**
3. Enter your T-Mobile phone number or account number
4. Choose an email address as your username
5. Create a strong password (at least 8 characters with letters and numbers)
6. Verify your identity via a text message sent to your T-Mobile phone
7. Complete the setup

### What you can do with your T-Mobile ID

- Log in to my.t-mobile.com
- Access the T-Mobile app
- Manage account settings and billing
- View usage and plan details
- Contact customer support

### Security tips

- Use a unique password that you don't use for other accounts
- Enable two-factor authentication for added security
- Set up your account PIN to protect against unauthorized changes
- Never share your T-Mobile ID credentials with anyone

title: Set up voicemail
content:
## Setting up voicemail on T-Mobile

### First-time voicemail setup

1. From your T-Mobile phone, **press and hold 1** or dial your own phone number
2. Follow the voice prompts to set up your mailbox
3. Create a **4-7 digit PIN** (avoid simple patterns like 1234)
4. Record your **personal greeting** or choose the default greeting
5. Your voicemail is now active

### Visual Voicemail

Most T-Mobile smartphones support Visual Voicemail, which lets you see and manage voicemail messages directly on your phone:

- **iPhone**: Open the Phone app, tap **Voicemail** tab
- **Android**: Open the Phone app, tap **Voicemail** or use the Visual Voicemail app

### Change your voicemail greeting

1. Press and hold 1 to call voicemail
2. Enter your PIN
3. Press **4** for personal options
4. Press **1** to change your greeting
5. Record your new greeting and press **#**

### Reset your voicemail PIN

If you've forgotten your voicemail PIN:
1. Dial **611** from your T-Mobile phone
2. Ask a representative to reset your voicemail PIN
3. Set up a new PIN when you next access voicemail

title: Understanding your first T-Mobile bill
content:
## Your first T-Mobile bill explained

Your first bill may look different from future bills. Here's what to expect.

### Why your first bill may be higher

Your first bill typically includes:
- **Partial month charges** from your activation date to the end of your billing cycle
- **One full month** of service charges in advance
- **One-time activation fees** (if applicable)
- **Device down payment** (if you purchased a device on an installment plan)
- **Taxes and fees**

### After your first bill

Starting with your second bill, you'll see a more predictable amount that includes:
- Your monthly plan charge
- Any device installment payments
- Add-on features
- Taxes and regulatory fees

### Save with AutoPay

Enroll in AutoPay with an eligible payment method to save up to **$5 per line per month** on select plans. AutoPay payments are withdrawn automatically on your due date.

### Questions about your bill

You can view a detailed breakdown of your charges in the T-Mobile app or at my.t-mobile.com under **Billing**.

title: Set up AutoPay
content:
## Setting up AutoPay

AutoPay automatically pays your T-Mobile bill each month and can save you money.

### AutoPay discount

With AutoPay using an eligible payment method (debit card or bank account), you can save **$5 per line per month** on select plans including Go 5G, Go 5G Plus, and Go 5G Next.

### How to set up AutoPay

1. Sign in to the T-Mobile app or my.t-mobile.com
2. Go to **Billing > Payment methods**
3. Tap **Set up AutoPay**
4. Add a payment method (debit card, credit card, or bank account)
5. Choose your payment date preference
6. Confirm enrollment

### Eligible payment methods for the discount

- Debit cards
- Checking accounts (ACH)

Note: Credit cards are accepted for AutoPay but do **not** qualify for the $5/line discount.

### Managing AutoPay

You can update your payment method, change your payment date, or cancel AutoPay at any time through the T-Mobile app or website.

### Important

- AutoPay processes on your bill due date
- Make sure your payment method has sufficient funds
- The AutoPay discount appears on your next billing cycle after enrollment

title: Understanding your T-Mobile plan
content:
## Understanding your T-Mobile plan

### Current T-Mobile plans

T-Mobile offers several plan tiers:

**Go 5G** — Our standard unlimited plan
- Unlimited talk, text, and data
- 5G access included
- 50GB premium data before potential deprioritization
- 5GB mobile hotspot
- Standard international texting

**Go 5G Plus** — Our popular premium plan
- Unlimited premium data (no deprioritization)
- 50GB high-speed hotspot
- Netflix Standard with Ads included
- International data and texting in 215+ countries
- In-flight Wi-Fi on select airlines

**Go 5G Next** — Our top-tier plan
- Everything in Go 5G Plus
- Apple TV+ included
- Device upgrade-ready every year
- 100GB high-speed hotspot

### Checking your plan details

1. Open the T-Mobile app
2. Tap **Account** then **Plan**
3. View your plan features, data usage, and included benefits

### Changing your plan

You can change your plan at any time through the T-Mobile app, online, or by visiting a store. Plan changes typically take effect at the start of your next billing cycle.

title: Getting started with 5G
content:
## Getting started with T-Mobile 5G

T-Mobile has America's largest and fastest 5G network.

### Types of T-Mobile 5G

**Extended Range 5G** — Our broadest 5G coverage
- Available in more locations, including rural areas
- Good speeds with wider reach
- Penetrates buildings better

**Ultra Capacity (5G UC)** — Our fastest 5G
- Available in select cities and areas
- Speeds that can rival home internet
- Ideal for streaming, gaming, and large downloads

### What you need for 5G

1. A **5G-capable device** (most phones released since 2021)
2. A **5G-compatible plan** (all current T-Mobile plans include 5G)
3. **5G coverage** in your area (check at t-mobile.com/coverage)

### Checking your 5G connection

Look for the **5G** or **5G UC** icon in your phone's status bar. If you see **LTE**, you may be in an area without 5G coverage, or your device may need a settings update.

### Tips for the best 5G experience

- Keep your device software up to date
- Make sure 5G is enabled in your device settings
- 5G UC performs best outdoors and in open areas
- Extended Range 5G works well indoors

title: Sprint to T-Mobile migration
content:
## Sprint to T-Mobile migration

Sprint customers have been migrated to the T-Mobile network. Here's what you need to know.

### What changed

- Your Sprint account has moved to the T-Mobile billing system
- You now have access to T-Mobile's 5G network
- Your phone number stays the same
- The Sprint app has been replaced by the T-Mobile app

### Setting up your T-Mobile account

1. Download the **T-Mobile app**
2. Create a **T-Mobile ID** using your phone number
3. Your account information and billing history have been transferred

### SIM card update

You may need a new T-Mobile SIM card to access the full T-Mobile network:
- Visit a T-Mobile store for a free SIM swap
- Or request one through the T-Mobile app
- eSIM activation is also available for compatible devices

### Plan options

You can keep your existing Sprint plan or switch to a current T-Mobile plan. Visit the T-Mobile app or a store to compare plan options and see if switching could save you money.

### Need help?

Former Sprint customers can get support through the same channels as all T-Mobile customers: the T-Mobile app, 611 from your phone, or visiting a T-Mobile store.

title: Setting up a family plan
content:
## Setting up a T-Mobile family plan

T-Mobile family plans let you share one account with multiple lines and save money.

### How T-Mobile family plans work

T-Mobile plans are priced per line, with discounts for additional lines:
- Lines 1-2 are priced individually
- Lines 3+ get automatic multi-line discounts
- Each line gets its own data, hotspot, and features

### Adding lines to your account

1. Sign in to the T-Mobile app or my.t-mobile.com
2. Go to **Account > Add a line**
3. Choose whether to bring a device or get a new one
4. Select the plan for the new line
5. Complete the activation

### Managing family lines

As the primary account holder, you can:
- Add or remove lines at any time
- Set different plans for each line
- Add authorized users who can make account changes
- Set up parental controls with FamilyWhere
- View data usage for each individual line

### Sharing the cost

The primary account holder receives one bill for all lines. You can view per-line charges in the T-Mobile app to split costs with family members.

title: Manage your T-Mobile account
content:
## Managing your T-Mobile account

You can manage your T-Mobile account online, through the app, or by contacting customer care.

### Online account management

At **my.t-mobile.com** or in the T-Mobile app, you can:
- View and pay your bill
- Change your plan or add features
- Update your personal information
- Add or remove lines
- Check device upgrade eligibility
- View data usage
- Set up AutoPay

### Account security

Protect your account by:
- Setting a strong **account PIN** (different from your T-Mobile ID password)
- Enabling **two-factor authentication** on your T-Mobile ID
- Adding **account verification** questions
- Reviewing **authorized users** regularly

### Contact customer care

- **Call**: 611 from your T-Mobile phone, or 1-800-937-8997
- **Chat**: In the T-Mobile app or at t-mobile.com
- **Visit**: Find your nearest T-Mobile store
- **Social media**: @TMobileHelp on X (Twitter)

Customer care hours: Available 24/7 for billing and account questions.

title: T-Mobile ID overview
content:
## T-Mobile ID

Your T-Mobile ID is your username and password for accessing your T-Mobile account online and in the app.

### What is a T-Mobile ID?

A T-Mobile ID consists of:
- **Username**: Your email address
- **Password**: A secure password you create
- **Phone number**: Linked to your T-Mobile account

### Creating a T-Mobile ID

1. Visit my.t-mobile.com or open the T-Mobile app
2. Tap **Register** or **Sign Up**
3. Enter your T-Mobile phone number
4. Choose your email as your username
5. Create a password
6. Verify via text message

### Using your T-Mobile ID

Your T-Mobile ID works across all T-Mobile services:
- T-Mobile app
- my.t-mobile.com
- T-Mobile Tuesdays
- T-Mobile MONEY
- Other T-Mobile partner services

### Security

- Your T-Mobile ID password is different from your account PIN
- Enable two-factor authentication for extra security
- If you suspect unauthorized access, change your password immediately and contact T-Mobile

title: Reset your T-Mobile ID password
content:
## Resetting your T-Mobile ID password

If you've forgotten your T-Mobile ID password, you can reset it easily.

### How to reset your password

1. Go to **my.t-mobile.com** or open the T-Mobile app
2. Click **Sign In**, then **Forgot Password?**
3. Enter your T-Mobile ID (email address) or phone number
4. Choose a verification method:
   - **Text message** to your T-Mobile phone
   - **Email** to your registered email address
5. Enter the verification code
6. Create a new password

### Password requirements

- At least **8 characters** long
- Must include **letters and numbers**
- Cannot be the same as your last 3 passwords
- Avoid using personal information like your name or phone number

### Locked out?

If you're completely locked out:

title: Remove a line from your account
content:
## Removing a line from your T-Mobile account

### Before you remove a line

- Check for any **device installment balances** remaining on the line — these become due immediately
- Review any **promotional credits** that may be affected
- Canceling a line during a billing cycle results in charges for the full cycle
- **Port your number** first if you want to keep it with another carrier

### How to remove a line

1. Call T-Mobile at **611** or **1-800-937-8997**
2. Verify your identity with your account PIN
3. Request line removal
4. Confirm you understand any remaining balances

Note: Line removal cannot be done through the app or website — you must contact customer care.

### After removing a line

- The line will be deactivated at the end of your current billing cycle
- You'll receive a final bill that includes any remaining device payments
- Your per-line pricing may change if you drop below certain line thresholds

title: Set up or change your account PIN
content:
## Account PIN

Your account PIN protects your T-Mobile account from unauthorized access.

### What is an account PIN?

Your account PIN is a **4-8 digit number** used to verify your identity when:
- Calling customer care
- Making account changes in-store
- Authorizing device upgrades
- Adding or removing lines

**Important**: Your account PIN is different from your T-Mobile ID password and your voicemail PIN.

### Setting up your PIN

1. Sign in to the T-Mobile app or my.t-mobile.com
2. Go to **Account > Profile > Security**
3. Select **Account PIN**
4. Create a 4-8 digit PIN
5. Confirm your PIN

### Changing your PIN

Follow the same steps above to update your existing PIN.

### Forgot your PIN?

- Sign in to my.t-mobile.com and reset it under **Profile > Security**
- Visit a T-Mobile store with a valid photo ID
- Call 611 and verify your identity through alternative security questions

### Security tips

- Don't use easily guessable numbers like 1234 or your birth year
- Don't share your PIN with anyone you don't trust with full account access
- Change your PIN periodically for better security

title: Understanding your T-Mobile bill
content:
## Understanding your T-Mobile bill

Your monthly T-Mobile bill includes several components. Here's a breakdown.

### Bill sections

**Account summary** — Total amount due and due date

**Plan charges** — Monthly cost for each line on your account, including any multi-line discounts

**Device payments** — Monthly installment payments for financed devices

**Features and add-ons** — Charges for additional services like device protection, international calling, etc.

**One-time charges** — Activation fees, upgrade fees, or other non-recurring charges

**Credits and adjustments** — Promotional credits, bill credits, or account adjustments

**Taxes, fees, and surcharges** — Government taxes and regulatory fees. On Magenta and Go 5G plans, most taxes and fees are included in your plan price.

### Viewing your bill

1. Open the T-Mobile app or go to my.t-mobile.com
2. Tap **Billing**
3. View your current bill summary or download a detailed PDF

### Bill due date

Your bill is due approximately **20 days** after the billing cycle closes. Set up AutoPay to ensure you never miss a payment.

title: AutoPay setup and management
content:
## T-Mobile AutoPay

AutoPay automatically pays your bill and can save you money each month.

### AutoPay discount

Save **$5 per line per month** when you enroll in AutoPay with an eligible payment method:
- **Eligible**: Debit card, bank account (ACH)
- **Not eligible for discount**: Credit card (AutoPay will still work, but no discount)

### Setting up AutoPay

1. Sign in to the T-Mobile app or my.t-mobile.com
2. Go to **Billing > AutoPay**
3. Add your preferred payment method
4. Review and confirm

### Managing AutoPay

You can:
- **Change payment method** at any time
- **Update payment date** preferences
- **Cancel AutoPay** if needed (you'll lose the per-line discount)

### Troubleshooting AutoPay

If your AutoPay payment fails:
- Check that your payment method has sufficient funds
- Verify your card hasn't expired
- Update your payment information if your bank issued a new card
- AutoPay will retry once before sending you a notification

### When does AutoPay process?

AutoPay processes on your bill due date. You'll receive a notification before each payment is processed.

title: Late payment and past due balance
content:
## Late payments on your T-Mobile bill

### What happens if your payment is late

1. **Grace period** — T-Mobile typically allows a short grace period after your due date
2. **Late fee** — A late payment fee may be applied to your next bill
3. **Service suspension** — After extended non-payment, your service may be suspended
4. **Account cancellation** — Continued non-payment may result in account cancellation and collection activity

### Making a late payment

If your payment is overdue:
1. Sign in to the T-Mobile app
2. Go to **Billing**
3. Pay the past-due amount immediately

### Payment arrangements

If you can't pay the full amount:
1. Open the T-Mobile app
2. Tap **Billing > Payment arrangement**
3. Choose a new payment date (up to 7-14 days from due date)
4. Confirm the arrangement

A payment arrangement helps prevent service interruption while you arrange payment.

### Restoring suspended service

If your service has been suspended:
1. Pay the past-due balance in full
2. Service is typically restored within a few hours
3. Call 611 from another phone if you need assistance

title: Billing cycle explained
content:
## Understanding your T-Mobile billing cycle

### How the billing cycle works

T-Mobile bills are generated monthly based on your billing cycle date. Your billing cycle:
- Starts on the same date each month
- Covers approximately **30 days** of service
- Is billed **in advance** (you pay at the start of each cycle)

### Key dates

- **Cycle start date** — When your billing period begins
- **Cycle end date** — When your billing period ends
- **Bill generated** — Your bill is created 1-2 days after the cycle ends
- **Due date** — Approximately 20 days after the bill is generated

### Finding your billing cycle dates

1. Open the T-Mobile app
2. Go to **Billing**
3. Your current cycle dates are shown on the billing summary

### Mid-cycle changes

If you make changes mid-cycle (like upgrading a plan or adding a line):
- **Prorated charges** may appear for partial month usage
- Plan changes typically take effect immediately
- You'll see the prorated amounts on your next bill

title: Account credits and promotional credits
content:
## T-Mobile account credits

### Types of credits

**Promotional credits** — Bill credits applied monthly as part of a deal or promotion (e.g., device trade-in credits, switching credits)

**One-time credits** — Applied to your account for service issues, billing errors, or goodwill adjustments

**Loyalty credits** — Special credits for long-term customers

### Viewing your credits

1. Open the T-Mobile app or go to my.t-mobile.com
2. Go to **Billing > Bill details**
3. Look for the **Credits and adjustments** section

### Promotional credit details

- Promotional credits are applied monthly over a set period (usually 24 or 36 months)
- Credits appear as line items on your bill
- Canceling the associated line or changing plans may cause you to lose remaining credits
- Credits start within 1-2 billing cycles after the promotion is activated

### Requesting a credit

If you believe you're missing a credit or were charged incorrectly:
1. Contact T-Mobile at 611 or through the app
2. Provide details about the promotion or issue
3. A representative will review and apply credits if applicable

title: International charges on your bill
content:
## Understanding international charges

### International services and charges

International usage can result in additional charges depending on your plan:

**Included with Go 5G Plus and Go 5G Next:**
- Data and texting in 215+ countries at no extra charge
- International calls at reduced rates

**Go 5G and Essentials:**
- International texting included
- International data available with add-on passes
- International calling charged per-minute

### Types of international charges

- **International calls** — Charged per minute at destination-specific rates
- **International roaming** — Data and calls used while abroad
- **International text messages** — May be included depending on plan

### Avoiding unexpected charges

- Check your plan's international features before traveling
- Enable **Wi-Fi calling** to make calls over Wi-Fi without roaming charges
- Purchase a **Travel Pass** or **International Pass** before your trip
- Turn off **data roaming** in your phone settings if you don't want to use cellular data abroad

### Viewing international charges

International charges appear as separate line items on your bill under **Usage charges**.

title: Taxes and fees explained
content:
## Taxes and fees on your T-Mobile bill

### Taxes and fees included plans

On many T-Mobile plans (Go 5G, Go 5G Plus, Go 5G Next), **taxes and fees are included** in your plan price. This means:
- The price you see is the price you pay
- No surprise charges at the end of your bill
- Government taxes and regulatory fees are covered

### Plans with separate taxes and fees

Some plans (Essentials, certain prepaid plans) have taxes and fees listed separately:
- **Sales tax** — Based on your billing address
- **Federal Universal Service Fund** — Supports telecommunications access
- **Regulatory fees** — State and local telecom fees
- **911 surcharge** — Supports emergency services

### Viewing taxes and fees

In the T-Mobile app or online:
1. Go to **Billing > Bill details**
2. Scroll to **Taxes, fees, and surcharges**
3. View individual line items

### Why taxes vary

Tax amounts depend on:
- Your billing address (state and local rates vary)
- The specific services on your account
- Any applicable exemptions

- **5G UC** icon = Ultra Capacity 5G
- **LTE** icon = 4G LTE connection

### Maximizing your Extended Range 5G experience

- Keep your device software updated
- Ensure 5G is enabled in your device settings
- Extended Range 5G works well both indoors and outdoors
- Contact us if you're in a covered area but not connecting to 5G

title: Wi-Fi calling setup and troubleshooting
content:
## Wi-Fi calling on T-Mobile

Wi-Fi calling lets you make and receive calls and texts over a Wi-Fi network when cellular coverage is limited.

### Setting up Wi-Fi calling

**iPhone:**
1. Go to **Settings > Cellular > Wi-Fi Calling**
2. Toggle on **Wi-Fi Calling on This iPhone**
3. Enter or confirm your emergency address
4. Tap **Enable**

**Android:**
1. Go to **Settings > Connections > Wi-Fi Calling** (may vary by device)
2. Toggle on **Wi-Fi Calling**
3. Enter your emergency address
4. Tap **Save**

### Benefits

- Make calls in areas with weak cellular signal but good Wi-Fi
- Calls and texts use your T-Mobile number
- No additional charges — Wi-Fi calls are treated as regular calls
- Works at home, at work, or anywhere with Wi-Fi

### Troubleshooting

If Wi-Fi calling isn't working:
- Make sure Wi-Fi calling is enabled in your device settings
- Restart your phone
- Disconnect and reconnect to Wi-Fi
- Check that your Wi-Fi network has a stable internet connection
- Some public Wi-Fi networks may block Wi-Fi calling

content:

- Turn off **data roaming** if you don't want to use cellular data abroad
- Use **Wi-Fi** whenever available
- Set up Wi-Fi calling to make calls over Wi-Fi without roaming
- Monitor your usage in the T-Mobile app

title: Troubleshoot network and connectivity issues
content:
## Network troubleshooting

If you're experiencing network issues, try these steps.

### Basic troubleshooting

1. **Toggle airplane mode** — Turn airplane mode on, wait 30 seconds, then turn it off
2. **Restart your phone** — A simple restart fixes many connectivity issues
3. **Check for outages** — Visit t-mobile.com/support or check the T-Mobile app for network alerts
4. **Check coverage** — Verify you're in a T-Mobile coverage area

### Advanced troubleshooting

5. **Reset network settings** — This clears saved Wi-Fi networks and cellular settings
   - iPhone: Settings > General > Transfer or Reset > Reset > Reset Network Settings
   - Android: Settings > System > Reset > Reset Wi-Fi, mobile & Bluetooth
6. **Remove and reinsert SIM** — Power off, remove SIM, wait 30 seconds, reinsert and power on
7. **Check for software updates** — Install any pending device updates
8. **Check APN settings** — Ensure your Access Point Name settings are correct for T-Mobile

### When to contact T-Mobile

If the above steps don't resolve your issue:
- Call **611** from your T-Mobile phone
- Chat with us in the T-Mobile app
- A T-Mobile Expert can run diagnostics on your line

title: Fix slow data speeds
content:
## Fixing slow data speeds

### Common causes of slow speeds

- **Network congestion** — Heavy usage in your area during peak times
- **Deprioritization** — On some plans, speeds may be reduced after using a certain amount of data
- **Weak signal** — Low signal strength affects data speeds
- **Device issues** — Outdated software or settings problems

### Steps to improve data speeds

1. **Check your data usage** — Open the T-Mobile app to see if you've hit any data thresholds
2. **Toggle airplane mode** on and off to refresh your connection
3. **Move to a different location** — Signal strength varies, especially indoors
4. **Connect to Wi-Fi** when available for the fastest experience
5. **Restart your device** to clear temporary network glitches
6. **Update your device** to ensure you have the latest software and network optimizations
7. **Reset network settings** as a last resort

### Data deprioritization

On Go 5G, after 50GB of usage in a billing cycle, your data speeds may be temporarily slower during network congestion. Go 5G Plus and Go 5G Next offer unlimited premium data with no deprioritization.

### Speed test

Run a speed test using a reliable app to check your current download and upload speeds. Typical T-Mobile 5G speeds range from 50-300 Mbps depending on location and network type.

title: No signal or service troubleshooting
content:
## No signal troubleshooting

If your T-Mobile phone shows no signal or "No Service," try these steps.

### Quick fixes

1. **Check airplane mode** — Make sure airplane mode is OFF
2. **Restart your phone** — Power off completely, wait 30 seconds, power back on
3. **Check coverage** — Verify you're in a T-Mobile coverage area at t-mobile.com/coverage

### SIM card issues

5. **Reinsert your SIM** — Remove, clean gently, and reinsert

7. **Check eSIM** — If using eSIM, verify it's active in Settings

   - Android: Settings > Connections > Mobile Networks > Automatic
9. **Reset network settings** — Clears all network configurations
10. **Check for updates** — Install any available software updates

### If nothing works

- Visit a T-Mobile store for hands-on troubleshooting
- Call **1-800-937-8997** from another phone
- Your device may need repair or replacement

title: Airplane mode explained
content:
## Airplane mode

### What is airplane mode?

Airplane mode disables all wireless communications on your device:
- Cellular (calls, texts, data)
- Wi-Fi
- Bluetooth
- GPS (on some devices)

### When to use airplane mode

- **On flights** when required by the airline
- **As a network reset** — Toggle on and off to fix connectivity issues
- **Battery saving** — Disabling radios extends battery life
- **Avoiding charges** — Prevent international roaming charges while abroad

### Using airplane mode

**iPhone:** Swipe down from top-right to access Control Center, tap the airplane icon

**Android:** Swipe down from the top to access Quick Settings, tap the airplane icon

### Wi-Fi and Bluetooth with airplane mode

You can enable Wi-Fi and Bluetooth individually while airplane mode is on:
1. Turn on airplane mode
2. Turn on Wi-Fi separately
3. Connect to available Wi-Fi networks
4. Wi-Fi calling will work if enabled

This is useful on flights that offer Wi-Fi service.

title: Report a network issue
content:
## Reporting network issues to T-Mobile

Help T-Mobile improve network coverage by reporting issues.

### How to report

**Through the T-Mobile app:**
1. Open the T-Mobile app
2. Go to **More > Network**
3. Tap **Report an issue**
4. Describe the problem (dropped call, no data, slow speeds)
5. Your location is automatically included

**By contacting support:**
- Call **611** from your T-Mobile phone
- Chat in the T-Mobile app
- Visit a T-Mobile store

### What to include in your report

- Your **location** (address or intersection)
- The **type of issue** (no service, dropped calls, slow data)
- **When it happens** (all the time, specific times, intermittently)
- Your **device model**

### What happens next

T-Mobile's network engineering team reviews reports and:
- Investigates the reported area
- Checks for equipment issues or outages
- Plans network improvements based on reported patterns
- May follow up if more information is needed

Network improvements are ongoing and your reports help prioritize upgrades in your area.

title: Tower outages and service interruptions
content:
## Network outages and service interruptions

### Checking for outages

To check if there's an outage in your area:
1. Visit **t-mobile.com/support** or open the T-Mobile app
2. Check for **network alerts** or **outage notifications**
3. Follow **@TMobile** on social media for status updates

### Types of service interruptions

**Planned maintenance** — Scheduled work to upgrade or maintain network equipment. Usually happens during low-traffic hours and is communicated in advance.

**Unplanned outage** — Caused by equipment failure, weather events, power outages, or physical damage to network infrastructure.

### During an outage

- **Wi-Fi calling** may still work if you have a Wi-Fi connection
- **Emergency calls (911)** are designed to work even during outages through partner networks
- Your phone may automatically connect to partner networks for basic service

### Outage resolution

T-Mobile works to resolve outages as quickly as possible. Resolution times depend on the cause:
- Equipment issues: Usually resolved within hours
- Weather damage: May take longer depending on severity
- Power outages: Resolved when power is restored

title: Change your T-Mobile plan
content:
## Changing your T-Mobile plan

### How to change your plan

**In the T-Mobile app:**
1. Sign in and go to **Account > Plan**
2. Tap **Change plan**
3. Browse available plans
4. Select your new plan
5. Review the changes and confirm

**Online:**
1. Go to my.t-mobile.com
2. Navigate to **Account > Plan**
3. Follow the same steps as above

**By phone or in-store:**
- Call 611 from your T-Mobile phone
- Visit any T-Mobile store

### When changes take effect

- **Upgrades** (moving to a higher-tier plan) — Take effect immediately
- **Downgrades** (moving to a lower-tier plan) — Take effect at the start of your next billing cycle

### Things to consider

- Changing plans may affect promotional credits on your account
- Check for any device installment requirements tied to your current plan
- Multi-line discounts may change if different lines have different plans
- Review the new plan's features to make sure it meets your needs

### Per-line plan changes

On family accounts, you can set different plans for different lines. Each line can have the plan that best fits that user's needs.

title: Mobile hotspot on T-Mobile
content:
## Mobile hotspot

Use your T-Mobile phone as a portable Wi-Fi hotspot to connect other devices.

### Hotspot data by plan

| Plan | High-speed hotspot |
|------|-------------------|
| Go 5G | 5GB |
| Go 5G Plus | 50GB |
| Go 5G Next | 100GB |
| Essentials | Unlimited at 3G speeds |

After using your high-speed allotment, hotspot continues at reduced speeds for the rest of the billing cycle.

**iPhone:**
1. Go to **Settings > Personal Hotspot**

### Tips for best performance

- Keep your hotspot device plugged in (hotspot uses significant battery)
- Place your phone where it gets the best signal
- Limit the number of connected devices for better speeds
- Connect to 5G for the fastest hotspot experience

title: Scam Shield call protection
content:
## Scam Shield

T-Mobile Scam Shield helps protect you from scam and spam calls.

### Scam Shield (included with all plans)

Everything in basic Scam Shield, plus:
- **Caller ID** — See the name and number of unknown callers
- **Reverse number lookup** — Search any phone number
- **Blocked call list** — View and manage blocked numbers
- **Personal block list** — Add specific numbers to block
- **Voicemail to text** — Voicemails transcribed to text

### Setting up Scam Shield

1. Download the **Scam Shield** app from the App Store or Google Play
2. Open the app and sign in with your T-Mobile ID
3. Enable the features you want

### Managing spam calls

In the Scam Shield app, you can:
- Adjust your blocking level (standard, aggressive, or custom)
- Report scam numbers
- View your call screening history
- Manage your personal block list

title: Call forwarding on T-Mobile
content:
## Call forwarding

Forward incoming calls to another phone number.

### Types of call forwarding

**Forward all calls** — All incoming calls go to another number
- Activate: Dial **\*72** + the forwarding number, then press **Send/Call**
- Deactivate: Dial **\*73** then press **Send/Call**

**Forward when busy** — Calls forward when your line is busy
- Activate: Dial **\*67** + the forwarding number, then press **Send/Call**
- Deactivate: Dial **\*87** then press **Send/Call**

**Forward when no answer** — Calls forward after a number of rings
- Activate: Dial **\*61** + the forwarding number, then press **Send/Call**
- Deactivate: Dial **\*83** then press **Send/Call**

**Forward when unreachable** — Calls forward when your phone is off or out of service
- Activate: Dial **\*62** + the forwarding number, then press **Send/Call**
- Deactivate: Dial **\*82** then press **Send/Call**

### Important

- Standard airtime charges apply to forwarded calls
- The forwarding number receives the call as a regular incoming call
- Call forwarding works with both domestic and international numbers (international charges may apply)

Visual Voicemail lets you view, listen to, and manage voicemail messages directly on your phone's screen.

Instead of dialing into a voicemail system, Visual Voicemail shows you:

**Android:**

With Scam Shield Premium, voicemail messages are automatically transcribed to text, so you can read your messages without listening.

### Troubleshooting

If Visual Voicemail isn't working:
- Make sure you have a cellular data or Wi-Fi connection
- Update your phone to the latest software version
- Restart your phone
- Contact T-Mobile if the issue persists

title: Install or replace a SIM card
content:
## SIM card installation

### What you need

- Your new T-Mobile SIM card
- A SIM eject tool (or a small paperclip)
- Your device

### Installing a SIM card

1. **Power off** your device
2. **Locate the SIM tray** — usually on the side of your phone
3. Insert the **SIM eject tool** into the small hole next to the tray
4. **Gently push** until the tray pops out
5. Place the **SIM card** in the tray with the gold contacts facing down
6. **Slide the tray** back into the phone
7. **Power on** your device

### SIM card sizes

T-Mobile SIM cards come in a multi-size format that can be punched out to fit:
- **Nano SIM** — Most modern smartphones
- **Micro SIM** — Some older devices
- **Standard SIM** — Rarely used in modern devices

### Getting a replacement SIM

If you need a new SIM card:
- Visit a **T-Mobile store** for a free replacement
- Order one through the **T-Mobile app** or website
- Call **611** to request one by mail

### What you need

- Your new T-Mobile SIM card
- A SIM eject tool (or a small paperclip)
- Your device

### Installing a SIM card

1. **Power off** your device
2. **Locate the SIM tray** — usually on the side of your phone

4. **Gently push** until the tray pops out
5. Place the **SIM card** in the tray with the gold contacts facing down
6. **Slide the tray** back into the phone
7. **Power on** your device

### SIM card sizes

T-Mobile SIM cards come in a multi-size format that can be punched out to fit:

- Call **611** to request one by mail

title: eSIM activation on T-Mobile
content:
## eSIM activation

eSIM (embedded SIM) lets you activate your T-Mobile service without a physical SIM card.

### Compatible devices

eSIM is available on:
- iPhone XR and later
- Google Pixel 3 and later
- Samsung Galaxy S20 and later
- And many other modern smartphones

### How to activate eSIM

**During device setup:**
1. When setting up a new device, select **Set Up eSIM** when prompted
2. Follow the on-screen instructions
3. Your T-Mobile service will be activated

**On an existing device:**
1. Open the T-Mobile app
2. Go to **Account > Manage device**
3. Select **Activate eSIM**
4. Follow the prompts to complete activation
5. Restart your device

**Using a QR code:**
1. Contact T-Mobile or visit a store to get an eSIM QR code
2. On your device, go to **Settings > Cellular > Add eSIM**
3. Scan the QR code
4. Follow the on-screen prompts

### Benefits of eSIM

- No physical SIM card to lose or damage
- Switch devices more easily
- Some devices support dual SIM (eSIM + physical SIM)
- Activate service instantly without waiting for a SIM card

### Troubleshooting

If eSIM activation fails:
- Make sure you're connected to Wi-Fi
- Restart your device and try again
- Contact T-Mobile for a new eSIM activation code

title: Transfer data between devices
content:
## Transferring data to your new device

### iPhone to iPhone

**Quick Start (recommended):**
1. Place your old and new iPhones close together
2. The new iPhone will detect your old one
3. Follow the prompts to transfer data directly
4. The transfer may take 30 minutes to several hours

**iCloud backup:**
1. Back up your old iPhone: Settings > [Your name] > iCloud > iCloud Backup
2. On your new iPhone, select **Restore from iCloud Backup** during setup

### Android to Android

**During setup:**
1. On the new phone, select **Copy apps and data**
2. Connect both phones with a USB cable, or tap **A backup from the cloud**
3. Sign in with your Google account
4. Select what to transfer

### iPhone to Android

1. On your new Android, select transfer during setup
2. Use the provided cable or follow wireless transfer prompts
3. Contacts, photos, and videos will transfer
4. Note: Some iOS-specific data may not transfer

### Android to iPhone

1. Download **Move to iOS** on your Android
2. During iPhone setup, select **Move Data from Android**
3. Follow the code matching process
4. Select what to transfer

### What transfers

- Contacts, photos, videos, and calendar events transfer between all platforms
- Apps may need to be re-downloaded on the new platform
- Some app data and settings may not transfer between iOS and Android

title: Device troubleshooting basics
content:
## Basic device troubleshooting

Most common device issues can be resolved with these simple steps.

### Step 1: Restart your device

A restart fixes many temporary issues:
- **iPhone**: Press and hold Side + Volume button, slide to power off, wait 30 seconds, power on
- **Android**: Press and hold Power button, tap Restart

### Step 2: Check for software updates

- **iPhone**: Settings > General > Software Update
- **Android**: Settings > System > Software Update

Installing the latest updates can fix bugs and improve performance.

### Step 3: Free up storage

Low storage can cause performance issues:
- Delete unused apps
- Clear app caches
- Move photos and videos to cloud storage
- Check storage: Settings > Storage (or General > iPhone Storage)

### Step 4: Reset network settings

If you're having connectivity issues:
- **iPhone**: Settings > General > Transfer or Reset > Reset > Reset Network Settings
- **Android**: Settings > System > Reset > Reset Wi-Fi, mobile & Bluetooth

### Step 5: Factory reset (last resort)

If all else fails, a factory reset restores your device to its original settings. **Back up your data first!**

### When to contact T-Mobile

If these steps don't resolve your issue, contact T-Mobile at 611 or visit a store. Your device may need repair or replacement.

title: How to restart your device
content:
## Restarting your device

Restarting (also called rebooting) your phone is the first troubleshooting step for most issues.

### iPhone

**iPhone with Face ID (X and later):**
1. Press and hold the **Side button** and either **Volume button**
2. Slide the **power off slider**
3. Wait **30 seconds**
4. Press and hold the **Side button** until the Apple logo appears

**iPhone with Home button (8 and earlier):**
1. Press and hold the **Side button**
2. Slide to power off
3. Wait 30 seconds, then press the Side button to turn on

### Android

1. Press and hold the **Power button** for 3-5 seconds
2. Tap **Restart** (or **Reboot**)
3. Wait for your phone to restart

### Force restart (frozen device)

**iPhone (Face ID models):** Quickly press Volume Up, then Volume Down, then hold the Side button until the Apple logo appears.

**Android:** Press and hold Power + Volume Down for 10-15 seconds until the device restarts.

### When to restart

- Phone is slow or unresponsive
- Apps keep crashing
- No cellular signal
- Bluetooth or Wi-Fi not connecting
- Battery draining unusually fast

title: Factory reset your device
content:
## Factory reset

A factory reset erases all data and returns your device to its original settings. Use this as a last resort for persistent issues.

### Before you reset

**IMPORTANT: Back up your data first!**

- **iPhone**: Settings > [Your name] > iCloud > iCloud Backup > Back Up Now
- **Android**: Settings > System > Backup > Back up now

Also:
- Note your Wi-Fi passwords
- Save your T-Mobile account PIN
- Sign out of your Google/Apple account
- Remove your SIM card if sending the device in for service

### How to factory reset

**iPhone:**
1. Go to **Settings > General > Transfer or Reset iPhone**
2. Tap **Erase All Content and Settings**
3. Enter your passcode
4. Confirm the reset

**Android:**
1. Go to **Settings > System > Reset**
2. Tap **Erase all data (factory reset)**
3. Enter your PIN/password
4. Confirm the reset

### After the reset

1. Set up your device as new or restore from backup
2. Re-download your apps
3. Sign back into your accounts
4. Re-enable Wi-Fi calling and other T-Mobile settings

### When to factory reset

- Persistent software issues not fixed by restart
- Severe performance problems
- Before selling or giving away your device
- Recommended by T-Mobile support

title: Unlock your T-Mobile device
content:
## Device unlock policy

T-Mobile may lock devices to the T-Mobile network when purchased. Here's how to get your device unlocked.

### Eligibility requirements

Your device can be unlocked if:
- The device has been **active on T-Mobile for at least 40 days**
- The device is **fully paid off** (no remaining installment balance)
- Your account is **in good standing** (no past-due balance)
- The device has **not been reported lost or stolen**

### How to request an unlock

1. Contact T-Mobile at **611** or **1-800-937-8997**
2. Request a device unlock
3. Provide your device IMEI (dial *#06# to find it)
4. T-Mobile will process the request

**For iPhones:** The unlock is applied remotely. Connect to Wi-Fi, and the unlock should activate within 24-48 hours. You may need to restart your device.

**For Android:** You may receive an unlock code or the unlock may be applied remotely depending on the device.

### Temporary unlock for travel

T-Mobile may provide a temporary unlock for international travel if you don't meet the full unlock requirements. Contact customer care to request.

### Prepaid devices

Prepaid devices must be active on T-Mobile for at least **12 months** before they can be unlocked.

title: T-Mobile device trade-in program
content:
## Device trade-in

Trade in your old device for credit toward a new one.

### How trade-in works

1. **Check your device's value** at t-mobile.com/trade-in or in the T-Mobile app
2. **Choose a new device** and apply the trade-in credit
3. **Send in your old device** using the prepaid shipping label provided
4. **Receive credit** once T-Mobile processes your trade-in

### Trade-in value

Your trade-in value depends on:
- Device model and age
- Device condition (working, cracked screen, etc.)
- Current promotions

### Device condition requirements

To receive full trade-in value, your device should:
- Power on and function normally
- Be free of cracks on the front screen
- Not have water damage
- Have Factory Reset Protection disabled

Devices in fair or poor condition may receive reduced credit.

### Promotional trade-in offers

T-Mobile frequently offers promotional trade-in values that exceed the standard value. Check t-mobile.com/offers or the T-Mobile app for current promotions.

### Important

- Back up and erase your data before trading in
- Remove your SIM card and case
- Ship within **30 days** of receiving your new device
- Keep your tracking number until trade-in is confirmed

title: T-Mobile device protection and insurance
content:
## Device protection

Protect your device from damage, loss, and theft with T-Mobile's protection plans.

### Protection 360

T-Mobile's comprehensive device protection includes:
- **Device replacement** for loss, theft, and damage
- **Same-day repair** for cracked screens (in select areas)
- **AppleCare Services** (for iPhones) or **Samsung Care** (for Samsung devices)
- **McAfee security** for mobile and home devices
- **Tech support** — Expert help for any tech issue

### Coverage details

- Deductibles range from **$0-$275** depending on the claim type and device
- Up to **3 claims** in a 12-month period
- Coverage starts from the day you enroll

### How to enroll

1. Add protection within **30 days** of activating a new device
2. Open the T-Mobile app or call 611
3. Go to **Account > Add-ons > Device Protection**
4. Select your protection plan

### Filing a claim

1. Visit **mytmoclaim.com** or call **1-866-866-6285**
2. Describe the issue (damage, loss, or theft)
3. Pay the applicable deductible
4. Receive a replacement device (typically next business day)

### Important

- Protection must be added within 30 days of device activation
- A police report is required for theft claims
- File claims promptly — there are time limits for reporting

title: File a device protection claim
content:
## Filing a device protection claim

If your device is damaged, lost, or stolen and you have T-Mobile device protection, here's how to file a claim.

### Before you file

- Verify you have active device protection on your line
- For theft claims, file a **police report** first
- Know your device model and phone number

### How to file a claim

**Online (fastest):**
1. Visit **mytmoclaim.com**
2. Enter your phone number
3. Select your claim type (damaged, lost, or stolen)
4. Answer a few questions about the incident
5. Pay the deductible
6. Choose delivery option

**By phone:**
- Call **1-866-866-6285**
- Have your account information ready

### Deductibles

Deductibles vary by device and claim type:
- **Screen repair**: $0-$29
- **Device replacement**: $99-$275

### Receiving your replacement

- **Standard delivery**: Next business day
- **Same-day repair** (cracked screens): Available in select areas

### After receiving your replacement

1. Transfer your data to the new device
2. Return your damaged device within **10 days** (if applicable)
3. Activate the replacement device on your T-Mobile line

title: Lost or stolen device
content:
## What to do if your device is lost or stolen

### Immediate steps

1. **Suspend your line** to prevent unauthorized use:
   - T-Mobile app: Account > Line > Suspend line
   - Call 611 from another phone
   - Visit a T-Mobile store

2. **Track your device**:
   - iPhone: Use **Find My** at icloud.com/find
   - Android: Use **Find My Device** at android.com/find
   - Samsung: Use **Find My Mobile** at findmymobile.samsung.com

3. **Lock your device remotely** using the tracking service above

4. **File a police report** if your device was stolen

### If you have device protection

5. **File a claim** at mytmoclaim.com or call 1-866-866-6285
6. Pay the applicable deductible
7. Receive a replacement device

### If you don't have device protection

- Purchase a new device through T-Mobile
- Bring your own compatible device
- Check if your homeowner's or renter's insurance covers electronics

### Protect yourself

- Change passwords for any accounts logged in on the lost device
- Monitor your T-Mobile account for unauthorized activity
- Contact your bank if payment apps were on the device
- Report the IMEI as lost/stolen to prevent it from being activated on other networks

title: Software updates for your device
content:
## Keeping your device updated

Software updates improve performance, fix bugs, and add new features.

### Checking for updates

**iPhone:**
1. Go to **Settings > General > Software Update**
2. If an update is available, tap **Download and Install**

**Android:**
1. Go to **Settings > System > Software Update**
2. Tap **Check for updates**
3. Download and install if available

### Automatic updates

Enable automatic updates to stay current:
- **iPhone**: Settings > General > Software Update > Automatic Updates
- **Android**: Settings > System > Software Update > Auto download over Wi-Fi

### Best practices

- Update over **Wi-Fi** to avoid using cellular data
- Keep your device **charged to at least 50%** during updates
- Updates may take **15-60 minutes** — plan accordingly
- **Back up your data** before major OS updates

### Carrier updates

T-Mobile occasionally pushes carrier updates that optimize your device for the T-Mobile network. These install automatically and improve:
- Network connectivity
- Wi-Fi calling
- VoLTE (Voice over LTE)
- 5G performance

title: Battery optimization tips
content:
## Optimizing battery life

### Settings to extend battery life

1. **Lower screen brightness** or use auto-brightness
2. **Enable Low Power Mode** (iPhone) or **Battery Saver** (Android)
3. **Turn off background app refresh** for apps you don't need
4. **Disable location services** for apps that don't need it
5. **Reduce screen timeout** to 30 seconds or 1 minute
6. **Turn off always-on display** if your phone has one

### Connectivity tips

- Turn off **Bluetooth** and **Wi-Fi** when not in use
- Use **airplane mode** in areas with no signal (searching for signal drains battery)
- Disable **5G** and use LTE if you don't need the fastest speeds

### Battery health

**iPhone:** Check battery health at Settings > Battery > Battery Health & Charging

**Android:** Check battery health at Settings > Battery (varies by device)

### When to replace your battery

- Battery health drops below **80%**
- Phone shuts down unexpectedly
- Battery drains significantly faster than when new
- Phone gets very hot during normal use

T-Mobile stores can help with battery replacements, or you can file a claim if you have device protection.

title: T-Mobile app overview
content:
## The T-Mobile app

The T-Mobile app is your all-in-one tool for managing your account, paying bills, and getting support.

### Key features

- **Bill pay** — View and pay your bill, set up AutoPay
- **Data usage** — Monitor usage for each line
- **Plan management** — Change plans, add features, manage add-ons
- **Account settings** — Update personal information, manage lines
- **Support** — Chat with experts, get troubleshooting help
- **Shopping** — Browse devices, accessories, and deals
- **T-Mobile Tuesdays** — Access weekly rewards and deals
- **Network** — Check coverage and report issues

### Download

- **iPhone**: App Store
- **Android**: Google Play Store

### Sign in

Use your T-Mobile ID (email and password) to sign in. If you don't have one, you can create it during your first login.

### Troubleshooting the app

If the app isn't working:
- Update to the latest version

title: Scam Shield app
content:
## Scam Shield app

The Scam Shield app gives you control over spam and scam call protection.

### Download

- Available on App Store (iPhone) and Google Play (Android)
- Free for all T-Mobile customers

### Features

**Free features (all customers):**
- Scam ID — See "Scam Likely" on suspicious calls
- Scam Block — Block known scam numbers
- Report spam numbers

**Premium features (Go 5G Plus, Go 5G Next, or $4/mo add-on):**
- Caller ID — See who's calling even if they're not in your contacts
- Reverse number lookup
- Custom block lists
- Category blocking (telemarketers, political calls, etc.)
- Voicemail to text transcription

### Setting up Scam Shield

1. Download the Scam Shield app
2. Sign in with your T-Mobile ID
3. Enable **Scam Block** to automatically block scam calls
4. Adjust your **blocking level** (standard, max, or custom)

### How it works

T-Mobile uses advanced AI and network-level technology to identify scam calls. When a scam call is detected:
- The call may be blocked before it reaches you
- You may see a "Scam Likely" warning
- The call may be sent directly to voicemail

title: FamilyWhere location tracking
content:
## FamilyWhere

FamilyWhere lets you locate family members' phones on your T-Mobile account.

### What is FamilyWhere?

FamilyWhere allows you to:
- See the real-time location of phones on your T-Mobile account
- Set up location alerts when family members arrive at or leave specific locations
- View location history

### Setting up FamilyWhere

1. Download the **FamilyWhere** app
2. Sign in with your T-Mobile ID (primary account holder)
3. Select the lines you want to locate
4. Each line will receive a notification that location sharing is enabled

### Pricing

- FamilyWhere is available for **$8.99/month** per account
- Locate all lines on your account with one subscription

### Privacy

- All users on located lines are **notified** that FamilyWhere is active
- Users **cannot be tracked secretly** — notification is required
- Any located line can see that they're being shared
- The primary account holder manages which lines are included

### Compatible devices

FamilyWhere works with most T-Mobile smartphones. The phone must be:
- Powered on
- Connected to the T-Mobile network or Wi-Fi
- Have location services enabled

## Account alerts and notifications

Stay informed about your T-Mobile account with customizable alerts.

### Available alerts

**Billing alerts:**

- Payment confirmation

**Usage alerts:**

**Account alerts:**

### Managing notifications

You can customize:
- Which alerts you receive
- How you receive them (app, text, email)
- Data usage thresholds that trigger alerts
- Per-line notification preferences

## T-Mobile Tuesdays

Every Tuesday, T-Mobile customers get free stuff and exclusive deals.

### How it works

1. Open the **T-Life app** (or T-Mobile Tuesdays app) on Tuesday
2. Browse available offers
3. Tap to claim your freebies and deals
4. Use or redeem before the offer expires

### Types of offers

- **Free items** — Food, drinks, subscriptions, and more from popular brands
- **Discounts** — Exclusive savings at partner retailers
- **Sweepstakes** — Enter to win prizes each week
- **Gift cards** — Occasional gift card giveaways

### Eligibility

- Available to all T-Mobile postpaid customers
- Each line on your account gets its own offers
- Must be claimed on Tuesday (offers expire)

### Past partners have included

- Dunkin'
- Taco Bell
- Shell gas
- Movie tickets
- Streaming service trials
- And many more

### Tips

- Enable notifications so you don't forget
- Check early on Tuesdays — some offers are limited quantity
- Some offers require a separate app or account to redeem

title: NameID caller identification
content:
## NameID

NameID identifies unknown callers so you know who's calling before you answer.

### What is NameID?

NameID shows you the name and location of unknown callers — even if they're not in your contacts. It's like caller ID for every call.

### Features

- **Incoming call identification** — See the caller's name, city, and state
- **Reverse lookup** — Search any phone number to find out who it belongs to
- **Spam warnings** — Get alerts about potential spam callers
- **Call category** — See if the call is from a business, individual, or potential scam

### How to get NameID

NameID is included with **Scam Shield Premium**, which is:
- **Included** with Go 5G Plus and Go 5G Next plans
- Available as an **add-on ($4/mo)** for other plans

### Setting up NameID

1. Download the **Scam Shield** app
2. Sign in with your T-Mobile ID
3. NameID features are automatically enabled
4. You'll start seeing caller names on incoming calls

### Privacy

- NameID only identifies callers to you — it doesn't share your information
- You can opt out of being identified by others through the Scam Shield app
